thread.title: Making America Florida Again? These Trump cabinet picks have Sunshine State connections
thread.title_full: Making America Florida Again? These Trump cabinet picks have Sunshine State connections

author: , The Florida Times-Union
title: Making America Florida Again? These Trump cabinet picks have Sunshine State connections
text:
Making America Florida Again? These Trump cabinet picks have Sunshine State connections When does the second Trump presidency begin? Inauguration Day is Jan. 20, 2025. C. A. Bridges USA TODAY NETWORK - Florida Show Caption
Hide Caption
Trump nominates Pam Bondi for AG hours after Gaetz drops out Former Florida attorney general Pam Bondi is nominated by President-elect Trump to U.S. attorney general. She was also one of his defense lawyers. President-elect Donald Trump has been rushing through his cabinet choices at blinding speed, but then he hasn't looked far beyond the state borders. Many of his wildly controversial nominees have Florida connections . A surprising number of the rest are television personalities or his own defense attorneys.
Even when he had to abruptly replace his pick for attorney general Thursday, embattled former U.S. Rep Matt Gaetz , he chose another person from the Sunshine State: former Attorney General Pam Bondi. Here's a look at the Floridians Trump has chosen for his Cabinet so far.
Chief of Staff: Susie Wiles (Jacksonville) Soon after the election, Trump named his senior campaign adviser and longtime political power in Florida Susie Wiles as his White House chief of staff, making her the first woman in history to hold the title under a sitting president.
Experience for the job: Wiles, a political consultant for then-Governor Rick Scott and Gov. Ron DeSantis, has managed Trump's campaign schedule and has been credited with helping his unexpected political comeback.
Attorney General: Former Florida AG Pam Bondi (Temple Terrace) Pam Bondi replaces Trump's first choice, U.S. Rep. Matt Gaetz of Florida, who withdrew his nomination as allegations of sexual misconduct were resurfacing.
Experience for the job: Bondi was Florida's attorney general for eight years alongside then-Gov. Rick Scott and championed the fight against opioids and prescription drug abuse while fighting against the Affordable Health Care Act and the legalization of gay marriage. She has long been a Trump loyalist and was one of his defense attorneys during his first impeachment trial.
Secretary of State: U.S. Sen. Marco Rubio (Miami) Sen. Marco Rubio , R-Florida , a former foe of Trump's, is the president-elect's choice to be the next Secretary of State. Rubio, a Cuban American, would be the first Latino to serve in the position.
Experience for the job: Rubio has deep foreign policy experience through his roles in the Senate Intelligence Committee and the Senate Foreign Relations Committee and although he began political life as a moderate and was once opposing Trump, he has converted into a close ally who was a finalist on Trump's vice presidential list this year.
National Security Advisor: U.S. Rep. Mike Waltz (St. Augustine) Mike Waltz , R-Florida , a decorated Green Beret combat veteran, has served three terms as a Florida congressman from Florida's 6th District and won a fourth in November.
Experience for the job: Waltz has held civilian positions at the Pentagon, served as chairman of the House Armed Services Subcommittee on Readiness in addition to the House Intelligence Committee and House Oversight and Accountability Committee, and has supported Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis' war on "wokeness" and Trump's "America First" view of foreign relations.
What is Project 2025? 5 ways its radical ideas are similar to Florida laws
U.S. Ambassador to Israel: Mike Huckabee (formerly Santa Rosa Beach) Former Arkansas governor Mike Huckabee spent nearly a decade as a Florida man in South Walton County and even reportedly considered running in the 2018 Florida gubernatorial race, Florida Politics reported at the time, before moving to Little Rock to be near family.
Experience for the job: The former governor is a Baptist minister, former Fox News host, and current host of a show on the faith-based Trinity Broadcasting Network who has run for the Republican presidential nomination twice, unsuccessfully. Huckabee draws from his evangelical faith in his support for Israel and has said “there’s really no such thing as a Palestinian.” He has been a major proponent of Israeli settlement in the West Bank.
Administrator for Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services: Dr. Mehmet Oz (Palm Beach) Dr. Oz , who owns a historic mansion in Palm Beach and a cattle farm in Okeechobee along with other commercial investment properties in Florida, according to Business Insider , is a cardiothoracic surgeon and TV show host who rose to fame through multiple appearances on The Oprah Winfrey Show. He became infamous for his recommendations for alternative medications and treatments despite the lack of scientific evidence, such as using a combination of pregnancy hormones and a 500-calorie diet to spur weight loss.
Experience for the job: Trump appointed Oz to the Presidential Council on Sports, Fitness and Nutrition in 2018 and 2020. During the COVID pandemic, Oz praised the antimalarial drug hydroxychloroquine to treat COVID-19, a theory that was widely debunked but spread by Trump and other Republicans.
Deputy Attorney General: Todd Blanche (Palm Beach) Todd Blanche , a new Florida man, recently bought a home in Palm Beach County near Mar-a-Lago.
Experience for the job: A federal prosecutor and white-collar defense lawyer, Blanche aggressively defended Trump in his New York hush money trial and in two federal criminal cases.
